text:
$GOOGL ENTERS THE AGENTIC AI RACE WITH GEMINI SPARK

Gemini Spark is a personal AI agent that can run 24/7 on dedicated virtual machines powered by Gemini 3.5, Google Antigravity and MCP integrations across Google and third-party tools.

$GOOGL just announced Gemini Omni which is its new AI video generation model built directly into Gemini.

Google calls it “our first step towards a model that can create anything from anything.”

$GOOGL is launching Docs Live in the U.S. this summer.

Users will be able to create and edit documents with voice directly inside Google Docs.

GEMINI MONTHLY ACTIVE USERS

• May 2025: 400M
• Sept 2025: 650M
• Dec 2025: 750M
• Apr 2026: 900M+

$GOOGL CEO Sundar Pichai says Google now processes more than 3 quadrillion tokens per month across its ecosystem.

That kind of token growth is the clearest proof that AI cycle is still supply-constrained with demand pulling on compute, memory, networking & power all at once.
Polymarket now shows a 57% chance that Anduril becomes a $100B company by the end of this year.

Andrej Karpathy announced he is joining Anthropic saying the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be “especially formative.”

This is a major AI talent win given he was a founding member of OpenAI and later led AI at $TSLA where he helped build the Autopilot vision stack.

My favorite days as a long-term investor are the red ones.

The same businesses get more interesting when prices go down and everyone else gets scared.

Elon Musk said “datacenters in space” are easier than people think noting SpaceX already has 10K satellites in orbit.

He said Starship could make orbital compute real infrastructure adding that “you have to go to space” to climb the Kardashev scale.

$OPEN launched “Cash Now, More Later” giving home sellers upfront cash while still letting them keep upside from the final sale.

The program plugs into RealScout’s 100K+ agent network letting agents access Opendoor offers without changing workflows.

$NVDA earnings tomorrow are an ecosystem event and basically a read-through on the entire physical AI supply chain:

• Cooling: $VRT
• Foundry: $TSM
• Optical: $COHR, $FN 
• Power: $MPWR, $NVTS
• Packaging: $AMKR, $ASX
• Memory: $MU, $SNDK, $STX, $WDC, SK Hynix
• Interconnect: $AVGO, $ALAB, $MRVL, $APH, $CRDO

Bill Ackman runs a $13.7B portfolio with ~77% of it in just 5 stocks: 

• $BN ~18%
• $AMZN ~17%
• $UBER ~16%
• $MSFT ~15%
• $META ~11%

The portfolio is basically a bet that the biggest AI winners will be dominant platforms already monetizing massive user & customer bases.
$NBIS CEO Arkady Volozh says the spread between 6% and 10% financing can impact margins by ~20% over a five-year AI infrastructure contract.

$NVDA hand-delivered the first Vera CPUs to Anthropic, OpenAI, SpaceX and Oracle Cloud.

Vera is the company’s first custom CPU built for agentic AI marking its push deeper into the full compute stack as demand expands across CPUs, GPUs, networking and memory.

$OUST partnered with Fujifilm to bring native color lidar to its Rev8 OS digital lidar family.

Combining 3D depth and color in one sensor should make the data more useful for mapping, robotics, AI training and object recognition.

Jensen Huang says $NVDA has “the largest supply chain in the world” but AI demand is still outstripping global capacity.

The bottleneck is memory, compute, power and the physical buildout required to turn AI demand into usable capacity.

$WRTH 

An options income strategy that sells short-term, far out-of-the-money strangles on large-cap, non-biotech stocks after major earnings-driven price gaps (up or down), seeking to harvest elevated option premiums and rapid time decay. The strategy pairs the strangles with deeply out-of-the-money call protection to guard against extreme upside tail risk.

Sunday night: "Time of the Essense" for Iranian deal ...S&P futures down

Two hours before open: Close to a deal ... S&P futures rally

Morning: Not close to a deal, S&P down

Aftenoon: Holding off on tomorrow's attack to get a deal, S&P recovers.

In total, GS projects $7.6 trillion in cumulative AI capex for compute, data centers and power from 2026 to 2031, with more than $5 trillion going towards compute.

$NVDA $AMD $AVGO

Goldman Sachs projects annual AI capex of $765 billion in 2026, growing to $1.6 trillion by 2031. 

2026’s figure includes $494 billion of capex for compute and $232 billion for data centers, scaling to $1.13 trillion for compute and $436 billion for data centers by 2031.

$NVDA $AMD $AVGO $GOOG $AMZN

The real risk to the AI economy lies in physical constraints of scaling – not in compute availability from $NVDA or $AVGO, not in Big Tech’s software capabilities – but in power availability and thermal infrastructure that were never designed for this magnitude of demand.
